threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it Stoke Newington instead of Stokey—only locals know it’s not a mouthful, it’s a vibe.
Caught a whiff of jollof from a late-night takeaway on the 59. You’ll never find better midnight fuel than that classic mix of spice and nostalgia.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a group of kids building a makeshift skate ramp in the alley, buzzing with laughter and music from the nearby café.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a techno party in an old underground bunker while a street art tour happens just outside. Perfectly Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re locals when they know to avoid Dolores Park on Saturdays. Those 'Instagrammers' just don’t get the chaos that unfolds.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 31 this morning, and the driver had to coax a lost dog off the tracks while everyone just sat there. Classic San Francisco chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but the real gems are the plaza conversations and the hidden tapas bars in the side streets.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones eagerly checking Google Maps while standing in the middle of a crosswalk, blocking the flow of locals who know all the shortcuts.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the bus stop signs are decorative instead of part of a complex transport game. Good luck deciphering the Arriva map!
Caught a glimpse of a fox darting through the bus stop's shadows last night. Only in Peckham do you get wildlife with your 24-hour routes.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a guy in a suit confidently ordering from a döner stand while balancing a toddler on his hip. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a café that's also an old tram depot; the coffee tastes better with the sound of rumbling trains in the background.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their calm reaction to the BART delays. If they shrug at a smoke scare at Civic Center, they’ve been through worse.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 5 late last night. A guy was singing opera to a packed bus, completely out of tune, but everyone was vibing. Only in San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about mosaic streets and bars, but they miss out on the hidden gardens where locals actually unwind away from the crowds.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot a recent arrival by their overzealous enthusiasm for trying to speak Catalan in cafes, often fumbling with the accents.
